meaning: v.窒息,哽住,使呼吸困难,阻塞n.窒息,阻气门
examples: Very small toys can choke a baby.
The brandy made him choke, but it restored his nerve.
The plant will soon choke ponds and waterways if left unchecked.
Residents claim they often choke on the dust in the air in the evenings.
Hearing this description, Ms. Parker doesn't choke on her Korean braised goat and dumplings.
